meta.title: Event Hero
meta.description: A full-width hero section for single event pages (The Events Calendar) that displays the event title, start/end date and time, venue name, excerpt, and an optional external link button with gradient background.

attributes.name: showExcerpt
attributes.description: Toggle to display the event excerpt below the title

attributes.name: useTopicColor
attributes.description: Use the event category accent color for the gradient instead of the page accent color

section.notes: Mirrors PostHero outer wrapper. Conditionally adds event category accent color class when useTopicColor is true.

topGradient.notes: Reuses Hero.css gradient system via the top-gradient class.

heroInner.notes: Wraps the background image and content layer.

backgroundImageMask.notes: Contains featured image with CSS mask gradient fade. Style attr: mask-image: linear-gradient(to bottom, black 40%, transparent 100%). Only rendered when featured image exists.

backgroundImage.notes: Featured image rendered via wp_get_attachment_image with role=presentation and empty alt.

contentLayer.classes: relative z-10 pb-8 sm:pb-16 pt-[calc(var(--header-height)+180px)] sm:pt-[calc(var(--header-height)+280px)]

contentWrapper.notes: Left-aligned content constrained to 700px max width, same as PostHero.

upperGroup.notes: Contains event category pills and event title.

categoryPills.notes: Event category pills from tribe_events_cat taxonomy. Each pill uses accent color classes.

categoryPill.classes: inline-flex items-center justify-center px-2 py-2 rounded-full bg-accent-lighter border border-accent text-sm font-medium uppercase leading-[1.1] whitespace-nowrap shrink-0 text-charcoal
categoryPill.notes: Same pill styling as PostHero. Conditionally adds per-category accent color class.

eventTitle.notes: Event title using the largest heading size.

excerpt.notes: Optional event excerpt, only rendered when showExcerpt is true and excerpt exists.

metaGroup.notes: Lower metadata group containing date/time, venue, and optional external link button.

dateTimeLine.notes: Event date and time display. Smart formatting: if start and end are same day, shows 'Date | Start Time - End Time'. If different days, shows 'Start Date/Time - End Date/Time'.

venueLine.notes: Venue name, only rendered when event has a venue assigned.

externalLinkButton.notes: External link button using btn-tertiary style. Only rendered when event has _EventURL populated. Opens in new tab with rel=noopener noreferrer. Includes sr-only text for accessibility.

mobile.behavior: Same layout as desktop. Reduced padding (pb-8 vs pb-16). Heading scales down via text-header-0 responsive sizing. Content fills available width within container.

tablet.behavior: SM breakpoint padding kicks in (pb-16, larger pt). Otherwise same as mobile.

desktop.behavior: Full layout with max-w-[700px] content area left-aligned. Full gradient and masked image visible.

emptyState.noFeaturedImage: Hero renders without background image - gradient only visible
emptyState.noExcerpt: Excerpt area not rendered (controlled by showExcerpt toggle)

dateFormatting.implementation: Use _EventStartDate, _EventEndDate, _EventAllDay meta. Use date_i18n() for locale-aware formatting. Compare start/end date (Y-m-d) to detect same-day. Check _EventAllDay for time display.

externalLink.behavior: When populated, render a btn-tertiary link that opens in a new tab. Label: 'Visit Event Website' or similar.

editorBehavior.dataSource: Fetches current post data from tribe_events post type via useSelect + coreStore + editorStore
editorBehavior.editorPreview: Shows live preview of event data including title, date/time, venue, excerpt, and external URL indicator

phpDataSources.description: All data is fetched from The Events Calendar post meta and related posts. This block runs in a tribe_events single context.

tsxDataSources.description: Editor fetches tribe_events post data using useSelect with coreStore and editorStore, similar to PostHero pattern.

fields.startDate: post?.meta?._EventStartDate (requires meta exposed in REST API)
fields.venueName: Separate useSelect to fetch venue post title by ID

tsxDataSources.notes: The Events Calendar registers meta fields in REST API. If meta is not available, the editor may need a custom REST API extension or fallback to placeholder text. The developer should check availability and implement fallback.

externalLinkButton.ariaLabel: sr-only text describing the destination, e.g., 'Visit Event Website: Event Name (opens in a new tab)'

dateTime.ariaLabel: Use <time datetime='...'> element with ISO format datetime attribute for machine-readable dates

decisions.decision: Reuse PostHero layout structure (gradient, masked image, left-aligned content at max-w-[700px])
decisions.evidence: Spec says 'inspired on the PostHero block'

decisions.decision: Replace author/date meta group with event-specific meta: date/time, venue, and external link button
decisions.evidence: Spec requires: event title, start/end date+time, location name, excerpt, external link button

decisions.decision: Smart date collapsing: same-day events show date once with time range; multi-day events show full start and end
decisions.evidence: Spec says 'if the start and end date are the same show only once'

decisions.decision: External URL button only renders when _EventURL meta is populated
decisions.evidence: Spec says 'a button if it has an external link'

decisions.decision: Keep event category pills using tribe_events_cat taxonomy (matching PostHero category pill pattern)
decisions.evidence: PostHero uses category pills with accent colors; FeaturedEvents block uses tribe_events_cat taxonomy

decisions.decision: Use btn-tertiary for external link button style
decisions.evidence: card-tribe_events.php uses btn-tertiary for event card buttons; consistent with theme button system

decisions.decision: Use _EventAllDay meta to determine whether to show times or just dates
decisions.evidence: Spec requires showing date and time; all-day events logically should not show time. TEC stores this in _EventAllDay meta.

decisions.decision: Wrap date/time output in <time> elements with ISO datetime attributes for machine readability
decisions.evidence: Standard accessibility practice for date/time content; no conflicting pattern in PostHero

testPage.notes: Test page requires actual tribe_events posts with varying configurations. The developer should create or identify existing events with: (1) minimal event (title + start date only), (2) full event (all fields including featured image, venue, excerpt, external URL, end date on different day), (3) same-day event with different start/end times, (4) event with very long title and excerpt. Since this block reads from the current post context (tribe_events single), the test page should either use the block on actual event pages or the developer should create mock events.

variations.demoDescription: Event with only title and start date. No featured image, no venue, no excerpt, no external URL.
Event with featured image, multi-day date range, venue, excerpt, and external URL.
Event where start and end are same day but different times. Date should collapse.
Event with very long title, excerpt, and venue name.
Event with all content but no external URL. Button should not render.
Event with useTopicColor enabled for category accent gradient.
